reasoning: The BTFP facility stopped accepting new loans on March 11, 2024, and all loans had a maximum term of one year. Accordingly, all loans matured and the last loan was repaid by March 11, 2025. Recent search confirms that the program is fully closed and the balance has reached zero. The latest available reading from late January 2026 sits exactly at 0.000. It is virtually certain that the balance on 2026-02-01 will also be 0.000. In order for the balance to 'have increased' by the resolution dates (Feb 8 and March 3, 2026), the Fed would have to reopen the BTFP and extend new loans under this exact same reporting series. Without a sudden, severe banking crisis and a specific political decision to resurrect the BTFP rather than relying on the discount window or a new facility, this will not happen. Therefore, the probability of an increase is functionally the baseline risk of an unforeseen reporting anomaly or a completely unexpected immediate crisis, which is near 0. I place higher uncertainty on the slightly longer horizon, assigning 0.02 for Feb 8 and 0.03 for March 3.
